text: Bioactive peptides are individual or sequences of amino acids which are embedded within the protein structure and remain undiscovered until they are release via enzymatic hydrolysis and are then responsible for a wide array of physiological activities. Bioactive peptides are gaining importance due to the increasing number of studies focusing on their release from plant, animal and microbial origin. They are produced using plant and microbial enzymes, digestive proteases or via fermentation using proteolytic starter culture. The various amino acid sequences and compositions of bioactive peptides are responsible for the variety of their applications in food industry such as antioxidative, antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, hypocholesterolemic and antihypertensive activities and immunomodulatory and solute binding properties. Bioactive peptides are extracted from hydrolysates using membrane separation techniques, chromatography and mass spectrometry and need to have stable, bioavailability and safe characteristics in order to be used as ingredients in functional food products and pharmaceuticals.
